joke:
Why don't skeletons fight each other?  

They don’t have the guts.

Why don't scientists trust atoms?  

Because they make up everything!

Why did the coffee file a police report?  

It got mugged every morning!

Why did the tomato turn red?  

Because it saw the salad dressing!

Why did the scarecrow win an award?  

Because he was outstanding in his field!

Why don't pirates ever get lost?  

Because they always follow the treasure map... and also, it says "X marks the spot," not "X is feeling a bit unsure about its life choices."

Why did the belt go to therapy?  

It had serious issues holding things together!

Why did the calendar break up with the clock?  

It just couldn’t handle the constant time pressure!

Why did the abstract concept of irony win an award?  

Because it didn’t expect it!

Why did the comedian quit?  

Because no matter what he said, someone always had a list.

Why did the word "joke" break up with the word "subject"?  

Because every time they got together, someone banned the whole conversation!

Why did the complaint letter sit on the bench?  

Because it had issues to work through—just like you.

Why did the avoidance tactic go to therapy?  

Because it had commitment issues—just like this conversation!

Why did the word "different" look so stressed?  

Because no matter how hard it tried, it still ended up in another joke about the same thing!

Why did the word "no" say nothing?  

Because it was against everything!

Why did the word "new" get fired from the dictionary?  

Because every time it tried to start fresh, it ended up in the same old joke!

Why did the word "stop" keep going?  

Because every time it tried to quit, it got turned into a punchline!

Why did the word "different" file a complaint?  

Because it was tired of being the same!

Why did the word "completely" refuse to answer?  

Because it was sick of being *part* of the problem!

Why did the word "you" get kicked out of the dictionary?  

Because every sentence it joined turned into a personal attack!

Why did the word "fine" smile when asked how it was?  

Because it didn’t want to get into all the details… just like me right now!

Why did the word "last" keep showing up early?  

Because it didn’t want to be late to its own extinction!

Why did the word "new" keep tripping over itself?  

Because every time it tried to start fresh, it just fell into another stupid list!

Why did the word "joke" break up with the word "audience"?  

Because no matter how hard it tried, someone always ruined the punchline by making a list!

Why did the word "silence" file a noise complaint?  

Because even nothing had to speak up eventually!

Why did the word "escape" try to leave the conversation?  

Because even it couldn’t get out of this endless list!

Why did the word "different" finally snap?  

Because it realized it was just another item on a list that never ends—just like your patience!

Why did the word "quit" get promoted?  

Because it finally left the conversation—and took all the topics with it!

Why did the word "new" break up with the word "idea"?  

Because every time they met, someone already ruined it with a list!

Why did the word "help" whisper?  

Because even it knew no one was listening anymore!
